title: 10 best herbal capsules for gerd

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with GERD because they contain a natural anti-inflammatory compound called gingerol, which soothes the digestive tract and reduces inflammation in the esophagus. This relief helps to alleviate symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux, allowing food to digest more efficiently and reducing the likelihood of stomach contents flowing back up into the esophagus. By calming the digestive system, herbal ginger capsules provide natural relief from GERD discomfort and promote a healthier gut overall.
remedy_properties: Digestive Stimulant Property: Ginger's digestive stimulant property helps to increase stomach contractions, which can help to move food through the digestive system more quickly and efficiently, reducing symptoms of GERD such as heartburn and regurgitation.

Anti-Inflammatory Property: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, can help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can contribute to the development of GERD symptoms like chest pain and difficulty swallowing.

Antispasmodic Property: Ginger's antispasmodic property helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to GERD symptoms such as bloating and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Shogaols: These ginger-derived compounds help alleviate GERD symptoms by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and relaxing the lower esophageal sphincter, thereby preventing stomach acid reflux.

Gingerols: Specifically, the 6-gingerol and 8-gingerol isomers exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the esophagus and stomach, thereby alleviating GERD symptoms.
Zingiberene: This sesquiterpene has been shown to have a relaxing effect on the lower esophageal sphincter, reducing the frequency and severity of acid reflux episodes in individuals with GERD.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The most commonly used part of ginger, rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate GERD symptoms.
Roots: Ginger roots, also known as rhyzomes, contain compounds like gingerol and shogaol that have anti-inflammatory and carminative effects, making them useful in managing GERD.

Barks: Although less commonly used, ginger barks have been traditionally employed in herbal medicine to treat digestive issues, including GERD, due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the zingiber officinale roots after 8-10 months of growth in a shaded environment.
Clean and dry the harvested zingiber officinale roots in a low-temperature oven at 40c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried zingiber officinale roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix 500mg of the zingiber officinale powder with 500mg of cellulose powder and 10mg of magnesium stearate.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the zingiber officinale mixture and seal them using a capsule sealer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with GERD because they contain compounds that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Glycyrrhizin, a key component of licorice root, has anti-inflammatory properties that help to neutralize acid reflux and alleviate heartburn symptoms. Additionally, licorice capsules may also help to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, allowing stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus more smoothly, reducing symptoms of GERD.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Helps to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive tract mucous membranes, which can alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and acid reflux.
Licoricidin: Acts as a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which can help to protect the esophageal lining from damage caused by stomach acid and reduce GERD symptoms.
Flavonoids: Possess anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm down inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, reducing the frequency and severity of GERD episodes.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Flavonoids (Glycyrrhizin): These compounds help reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the esophagus, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with GERD.

Isoflavones: Isoflavones, specifically isoflavonoids like glabridin, have anti-inflammatory properties that may help protect the esophageal mucosa and reduce symptoms of GERD such as heartburn and acid reflux.
Saponins: Saponins, particularly glycyrrhizin, exhibit a mild anti-spasmodic effect, which can help relax the lower esophageal sphincter and reduce the frequency and severity of acid reflux episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are primarily used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps reduce inflammation and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Barks: The barks of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used due to their ability to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, providing relief from GERD symptoms.
Stems: The stems of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used due to their rich content of glycosides and flavonoids, which help to protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract and alleviate GERD symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra, clean and dry them in a low temperature oven for 24 hours.
Grind 20g of the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix the powdered glycyrrhiza glabra with 10g of starch and 2g of gelatin in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 500mg of the mixture and seal them using a capsule filler for 10 minutes.
Label the capsules with the name, dosage, and preparation date, and store them in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon capsules helps with GERD because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. The capsules also contain a compound called cinnamaldehyde, which has been shown to relax the muscles in the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing acid reflux and reducing symptoms of heartburn and bloating. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's ability to regulate blood sugar levels can help alleviate other GERD symptoms such as belching and flatulence, providing relief for those struggling with this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Cinnamaldehyde: This compound has natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with GERD symptoms such as heartburn and acid reflux.

Antioxidants: Ceylon cinnamon contains a high amount of antioxidants that can help neutralize free radicals in the body, reducing oxidative stress and damage to the esophageal lining, which can exacerbate GERD symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The capsules contain other anti-inflammatory compounds like cinnamic acid and epicatechin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of GERD by relaxing the lower esophageal sphincter and reducing stomach acid production.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: This phenolic aldehyde has natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Eugenol: A phenolic compound that has been shown to inhibit the production of stomach acid and relax the lower esophageal sphincter, thereby reducing the risk of acid reflux.

Cinnamic acid: A phenolic acid that has been found to have a relaxing effect on the muscles in the digestive tract, including the lower esophageal sphincter, which can help prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.
remedy_parts: Barks: Cinnamomum verum bark is commonly used to make capsules for GERD due to its anti-inflammatory and antacid properties.
Leaves: Cinnamomum verum leaves are used to make capsules for GERD as they contain compounds that help in reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.
Rhyzomes: Cinnamomum verum rhyzomes are used to make capsules for GERD as they contain cinnamaldehyde, a compound that has natural antacid properties and helps in soothing the digestive system.
remedy_recipe: Grind 20 grams of dried cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 50 grams of gelatin in a bowl to create a uniform blend.
Add 100 ml of distilled water to the bowl and stir the mixture until the gelatin dissolves completely.
Transfer the mixture to a capsule machine and fill 100 capsules with the herbal mixture.
Store the capsules in a cool dry place for at least 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with GERD because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and acid reflux. The curcumin present in turmeric also helps to strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, turmeric's natural antioxidant properties may help to soothe and protect the lining of the digestive tract, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with GERD.
remedy_properties: Curcuminoid compounds: Turmeric contains curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of GERD such as heartburn and acid reflux.

Cyclodextrin complexation: The curcuminoids in turmeric capsules are often formulated with cyclodextrin, a natural substance that enhances bioavailability by increasing absorption rates, allowing for more effective delivery of the active compounds to the targeted areas in the body.

Gastroprotective effects: Turmeric has been shown to possess gastroprotective properties, which can help reduce damage to the esophageal mucosa and protect against acid-induced injury, thereby alleviating symptoms of GERD such as pain, discomfort, and bleeding.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, helps with GERD by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the esophagus, thereby alleviating symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, another polyphenolic compound, has been shown to decrease acid production in the stomach, which can help alleviate GERD symptoms by reducing the amount of acid that flows back up into the esophagus.

Bis-demethoxycurcumin (BDMC): BDMC, a polyphenolic compound, helps with GERD by relaxing the lower esophageal sphincter (LES) and reducing acid production in the stomach, which can help alleviate symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Turmeric Rhizome Extract - It's used due to its high content of curcumin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate GORD symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Turmeric Rhizome Powder - It's used because it contains a significant amount of curcuminoids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve symptoms associated with GERD.
Rhyzomes: Curcuma Longa Rhizome Extract - It's used for its potential to reduce acid production in the stomach, alleviate heartburn and other GORD symptoms, and promote digestive health.
remedy_recipe: Collect 250g of dried turmeric roots and sift them through a fine-mesh sieve to remove lumps.
Grind the sifted turmeric roots into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the ground powder with 1 tablespoon of gelatin and 1 tablespoon of water in a small bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 5 minutes while stirring constantly until the gelatin dissolves completely.
Allow the mixture to cool and harden at room temperature for 30 minutes before filling capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel capsules helps with GERD because of its natural ability to soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation. The herb contains a compound called anethole, which has been shown to relax the muscles in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of acid reflux. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to expel gas and bloating, allowing for more efficient digestion and reduced pressure on the lower esophageal sphincter, thereby alleviating GERD symptoms such as heartburn and regurgitation.
remedy_properties: Carminative and anti-spasmodic properties: Fennel capsules help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and cramps that can contribute to GERD symptoms such as heartburn and acid reflux.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's antioxidants help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the esophageal lining and exacerbate GERD symptoms, while its anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.

Gastric motility regulation: Fennel capsules help regulate the movement of food through the digestive system, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and reducing the severity of GERD symptoms such as bloating and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole has anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of GERD by promoting the relaxation of the lower esophageal sphincter.

Ferulic acid: Ferulic acid has antioxidant and gastroprotective properties, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the stomach and esophagus, thereby alleviating symptoms of GERD and promoting healing of the esophageal mucosa.
Trans-anethole: Trans-anethole has antispasmodic properties, which help to relax the muscles of the lower esophageal sphincter and reduce symptoms of GERD, such as heartburn and acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds are often used for their carminative and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms of GERD.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate GERD symptoms.
Roots: The roots are utilized for their anti-inflammatory and carminative properties, which help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing GERD symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30g of foeniculum vulgare roots and rhizomes at the end of the blooming season.
Dry the harvested material in a well-ventilated area at 50-60°c for 2-3 days.
Grind the dried foeniculum vulgare roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1-2g of the fine powder with 1-2g of gelatin or vegetarian capsule material.
Fill the mixture into empty capsules using a capsule filler machine and store in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with GERD because they calm the lower esophageal sphincter, reducing inflammation and relaxing the digestive tract. The menthol in peppermint oil also decreases acid production in the stomach, which can help alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and regurgitation. Additionally, peppermint's natural anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation in the esophagus, making it an effective complementary treatment for managing GERD symptoms and promoting overall digestive health.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol's analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate heartburn symptoms by calming the muscle contractions that can exacerbate GERD.

Cineole: Cineole, a major constituent of peppermint oil, has a relaxing effect on the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, which helps to reduce spasms and alleviate symptoms such as bloating and discomfort associated with GERD.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a polyphenol present in peppermint, has antioxidant properties that help to protect the esophagus from damage caused by acid reflux, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: A phenolic diterpenoid in Mentha x piperita, rosmarinic acid helps with GERD by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and protecting against acid reflux.
Menthol: A monoterpenoid in Mentha x piperita, menthol aids in GERD by relaxing the muscles in the esophagus, reducing inflammation, and improving digestion.
Limonene: A terpene in Mentha x piperita, limonene helps with GERD by reducing inflammation in the stomach and esophagus, promoting digestion, and alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain menthol and menthone, which have antispasmodic properties that help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of GERD.
Stems: They contain essential oils, including menthone and limonene, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects that can help alleviate GERD symptoms.
Roots: They contain menthol and menthone, which can help soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, providing relief from GERD symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of mentha x piperita leaves and flowers when they are in full bloom for 3 weeks.
Dry the harvested mentha x piperita in a single layer at 30 degrees celsius for 2 weeks.
Grind 2 grams of dried mentha x piperita into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 2 grams of the powder with 6 grams of gelatin and 2 grams of glycerin to create a uniform blend.
Fill gelatin capsules with the mentha x piperita blend using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with Gerd because they provide natural relief from heartburn and acid reflux. The mucilages in marshmallow root coat and soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by stomach acid. This helps to calm the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of Gerd, such as burning sensations and regurgitation. Additionally, marshmallow's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce swelling in the esophagus, further alleviating discomfort and promoting a sense of relief.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Marshmallow root contains a high concentration of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that coats the esophagus and stomach lining, providing relief from inflammation and discomfort associated with GERD.

Soothing and calming effect: The mucilage in marshmallow root also has a soothing and calming effect on the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by acid reflux and heartburn, thereby alleviating symptoms of GERD.

Natural anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow root contains flavonoids and polyphenols that have natural anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, further alleviating symptoms of GERD.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help to form a protective barrier in the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with GERD.
Tannins: Tannins have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach, which can help alleviate GERD symptoms.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly kaempferol and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the esophagus and stomach, making them beneficial for GERD management.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Althaea officinalis are primarily used due to their high mucilage content, which can help soothe and protect the digestive tract.
Leaves: Althaea officinalis leaves are also used, as they contain mucilages that can help calm and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, reducing symptoms of GERD.
Barks: The bark of Althaea officinalis is sometimes used, as it contains similar mucilages to the roots and leaves, which can help soothe and protect the digestive tract and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of althaea officinalis when they are at their peak in the fall season.
Clean the roots thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris from the harvesting process.
Dry the roots in a well-ventilated area at a temperature of 50-60°c for 2-3 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a mill.
Fill gelatin capsules with 300-500mg of the powdered root and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kava capsules helps with GERD because it relaxes the lower esophageal sphincter, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux. The herb's anxiolytic properties also help alleviate stress-induced symptoms, such as tightness in the chest and difficulty swallowing. Additionally, kava's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling in the esophagus and stomach lining, preventing further irritation and damage from acidic stomach contents. This natural approach can provide relief for those suffering from chronic GERD symptoms.
remedy_properties: Kavalactones: Kavalactones are a group of compounds found in kava root that have been shown to relax the lower esophageal sphincter (LES), which helps to prevent stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and reducing symptoms of GERD.
Anxiolytic properties: Herbal kava capsules have anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) effects, which can help to reduce stress and anxiety that can exacerbate GERD symptoms such as heartburn and chest pain.

Muscle relaxant properties: Kavalactones also have muscle-relaxing effects on the smooth muscles of the digestive tract, including the esophagus and stomach, which can help to reduce spasms and contractions that can contribute to GERD symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Phenylpropanoids: These compounds, particularly kavalactones, have been shown to relax the lower esophageal sphincter (LES) and reduce inflammation, which can help alleviate symptoms of GERD.

Kavalactones: Specifically, kavalactones like kavalactone, kavain, and desmethoxyyangonin have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the esophageal lining from acid damage and reduce symptoms of GERD.

Flavonoids: Piper methysticum contains flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and protect the esophageal lining from acid damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Piper methysticum are commonly used due to their high concentration of kavalactones, which contribute to the plant's medicinal properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Piper methysticum are also used, but to a lesser extent compared to the roots.
Stems: Some sources suggest that the stems of Piper methysticum may be used in traditional medicine, possibly due to their kavalactone content.
remedy_recipe: Harvest mature piper methysticum roots and rhizomes for medicinal use within 3-5 years after planting.
Dry the harvested roots and rhizomes using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried piper methysticum into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or herb mill.
Mix the powder with a suitable capsule filler like cellulose or starch in a 1:2 ratio by weight.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine or spoon.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with GERD because of its unique ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms associated with acid reflux. The herb has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the esophageal lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by stomach acid backing up into the throat. Additionally, dandelion's bitter compounds can help stimulate the production of digestive enzymes, improving digestion and reducing symptoms of heartburn and regurgitation.
remedy_properties: Bitters: Dandelion root contains bitter compounds that stimulate digestive enzymes and improve gut motility, reducing symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn by promoting efficient digestion and emptying of the stomach.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion extracts contain a range of anti-inflammatory compounds, including flavonoids and terpenes, which help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of GERD such as chest pain and discomfort.
Alkalinity: Dandelion root has an alkaline effect on the body, helping to neutralize excess stomach acid and reduce symptoms of acid reflux by counteracting the acidity of the stomach contents.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpene compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate GERD symptoms.

Luteolin: A flavonoid phenolic compound, luteolin has been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help protect the esophagus from acid damage and reduce inflammation associated with GERD.

Apolactone: A triterpenoid compound, apolactone has been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help soothe the esophagus, reduce inflammation, and alleviate GERD symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain taraxasterol and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which help in soothing GERD symptoms.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds, they aid in reducing inflammation and providing relief from heartburn and acid reflux.
Seeds: Containing saponins, they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help in reducing GERD symptoms and promoting digestive health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale roots and leaves at the end of the blooming season when they are most potent.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for two weeks.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered taraxacum officinale with a binding agent like gelatin or vegetarian capsule filler in a ratio of 1:10.
Fill empty gelatin or vegetarian capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filler and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle capsules helps with GERD because of its potent antioxidant properties, which help soothe and protect the esophageal lining. The capsule's active compound, silymarin, has been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation caused by acid reflux, allowing the stomach contents to pass through the esophagus without discomfort. Additionally, milk thistle capsules may also help strengthen the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and alleviating symptoms of heartburn and regurgitation.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin is a flavonoid compound found in milk thistle seeds that has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the esophageal lining from damage caused by stomach acid reflux and reduce inflammation associated with GERD.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in milk thistle capsules have been shown to have a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms that can contribute to GERD symptoms, such as heartburn and regurgitation.

Silychristin: Silychristin is another key component of silymarin that has been found to have a protective effect on the esophageal mucosa, helping to prevent damage from stomach acid and reducing the risk of inflammation and scarring associated with GERD.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Silymarin is a flavonoid complex that helps protect the esophageal mucosa from acid damage, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with GERD.
Silibinin: Silibinin, a major component of silymarin, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce acid production and alleviate symptoms of GERD.

Flavonoids (including isosilybin and isosilibinin): These flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help protect the esophageal mucosa from acid damage, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of GERD.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used to make capsules due to their high content of silymarin, a compound that helps protect the stomach lining and reduce inflammation associated with GERD.
Flowers: They are used to make capsules due to their ability to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing symptoms of GERD.
Leaves: They are used to make capsules due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation and discomfort in the esophagus and stomach associated with GERD.
remedy_recipe: Crush 500 grams of dried silybum marianum plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 25 grams of the powdered plant material with 250 grams of gelatin to create a uniform blend.
Add 125 milliliters of distilled water to the mixture and heat it gently for 10 minutes to dissolve the gelatin.
Pour the mixture into empty gelatin capsules and seal them immediately to preserve the contents.
Label the capsules with the contents and date of preparation and store them in a cool dry place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper capsules helps with Gerd because they contain powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can alleviate symptoms of acid reflux. The capsules also provide a natural barrier against stomach acid, allowing for more efficient digestion and reducing the likelihood of acid escaping into the esophagus and causing discomfort. Additionally, bell pepper's anti-inflammatory properties may help to soothe and calm the digestive tract, further reducing Gerd symptoms.
remedy_properties: Ginger: Has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of heartburn.
Licorice root: Contains compounds that can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach and esophagus, reducing inflammation and irritation.
Marshmallow root: Has demulcent properties that can help create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with GERD.
remedy_parts: Roots: Bell pepper roots have been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues, making them a suitable component for medicinal capsules to alleviate GERD symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of the bell pepper plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, providing relief from GERD discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried capsicum annuum powder from organic sources.
Grind 500mg of licorice root powder and mix with 250mg of ginger powder.
Combine 500mg of the capsicum annuum powder mixture with 250mg of the licorice-ginger blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the capsicum-licorice-ginger mixture and seal immediately.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dog holly capsules helps with GERD because they contain a unique blend of herbs that work together to soothe and protect the digestive tract. The active ingredients, such as Marshmallow root and Licorice root, form a protective barrier in the stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by acid reflux. Additionally, the capsules' anti-inflammatory properties help to relax the esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and alleviating symptoms of GERD.
remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins, a type of bioactive compound found in Herbal Dog Holly Capsules, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax the lower esophageal sphincter (LES), which can help alleviate symptoms of GERD by reducing acid reflux.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in these capsules exhibit antioxidant properties, which help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by gastric acid and reduce inflammation, thereby providing relief from GERD symptoms such as heartburn and regurgitation.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, another class of bioactive compounds found in Herbal Dog Holly Capsules, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antacid properties, which can help neutralize stomach acid and reduce the risk of esophageal damage associated with GERD.
remedy_parts: Roots: Dog holly roots have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat digestive issues like acid reflux, due to their anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties.
Barks: The barks of dog holly contain bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and relieve symptoms associated with GERD, such as heartburn and regurgitation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ruscus aculeatus leaves and clean them thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned leaves using air circulation for 3-4 hours or until they reach 10% moisture content.
Grind dried ruscus aculeatus leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milligrams of powdered ruscus aculeatus with 1000 milligrams of vegetable fiber in a small bowl to create the capsule filling.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with GERD because they contain natural antacids and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation. The capsules' active ingredients, such as flavonoids and terpenes, work to neutralize gastric acid and alleviate heartburn symptoms. Additionally, elder's bioactive compounds enhance digestive enzymes' activity, promoting healthy digestion and reducing symptoms of GERD. By supporting the body's natural healing processes, elder capsules can provide relief from frequent acid reflux episodes and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Elder berries contain tannins that help to reduce inflammation and soothe the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, providing relief from acid reflux symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in elder capsules help to neutralize free radicals that can damage the lining of the esophagus and contribute to chronic inflammation, reducing the risk of GERD complications.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Elder berries contain flavonoids and polyphenols that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms such as heartburn and bloating associated with GERD.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and protect the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of GERD.
Rhyzomes: Elder rhyzomes contain compounds that help calm inflammation and reduce acid production in the stomach, providing relief from GERD symptoms.
Barks: Elder barks have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach lining, alleviating GERD symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried sambucus nigra leaves and flowers measuring 1000mg.
Grind the harvested plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the powdered herb with gelatin in a 1:5 ratio and add water to form a smooth paste.
Fill empty capsules with the herbal mixture, aiming for a total weight of 500mg per capsule after drying for 2 hours.
Store the sambucus nigra capsules in an airtight container at room temperature, using within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender capsules helps with GERD because they possess natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and irritation associated with acid reflux. The capsules also exhibit a calming effect on the muscles of the esophagus, allowing food to pass through more easily and preventing stomach contents from flowing back up into the esophagus. Additionally, lavender's antimicrobial properties help eliminate bad bacteria that can exacerbate GERD symptoms, promoting a healthy gut environment.
remedy_properties: Antispasmodic properties: Lavender's antispasmodic effects can help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and discomfort associated with GERD.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which is a common underlying cause of GERD symptoms.
Calming and anxiolytic properties: Lavender's calming effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are often triggers for GERD symptoms like heartburn and indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lavender roots are known to possess anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with GERD.
Seeds: Lavender seeds have been traditionally used to calm digestive issues, including heartburn and indigestion, which are common symptoms of GERD.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated containing 2-3% essential oils.
Weigh out 100g of dried flowers and transfer them to a clean glass container for future use.
Soak the dried flowers in 200ml of boiling distilled water for 15 minutes allowing for extraction of bioactive compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another container discarding solids after 10 minutes.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 500mg of the cooled lavandula angustifolia extract and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with Gerd because they contain apigenin, an antioxidant that relaxes the lower esophageal sphincter, allowing stomach acid to flow back up into the esophagus and reducing inflammation in the esophageal lining. Additionally, chamomile has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of heartburn and indigestion. By calming the digestive system, chamomile capsules can provide natural relief from Gerd symptoms, promoting a healthy and comfortable digestive experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can alleviate GERD symptoms such as heartburn and regurgitation.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in chamomile capsules neutralize free radicals that can damage the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing oxidative stress and improving overall gut health, which can help manage GERD symptoms.

Relaxant property: Chamomile's relaxant compounds, such as apigenin and bisabolol, calm the smooth muscle in the esophagus and stomach, relaxing the lower esophageal sphincter and reducing the frequency of acid reflux episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin and luteolin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe digestive issues associated with GERD.
Flowers: Known for their calming effects on the digestive system, chamomile flowers can help reduce inflammation and discomfort caused by acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Dry matricaria chamomilla flowers by spreading them in a single layer on paper towels for 24 hours.
Grind dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Sift ground matricaria chamomilla powder through a fine-mesh strainer to remove any lumps or large particles.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500-750 mg of sifted matricaria chamomilla powder and seal immediately.
Store filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey capsules helps with GERD because they contain allantoin, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that soothes and protects the esophageal lining. Allantoin also promotes healing and reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux such as heartburn and regurgitation. Additionally, comfrey's mucilages help to form a protective barrier in the stomach, preventing acid from flowing back up into the esophagus and reducing discomfort. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to treat digestive issues, including GERD, offering a safe and effective alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_properties: Allantoin: Comfrey contains allantoin, a compound that helps to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with acid reflux.

Saponins: The saponins present in comfrey have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the inflammation caused by GERD, promoting healing and relief from symptoms such as heartburn and bloating.

Alkaloids: Comfrey's alkaloid content helps to stimulate the production of mucin, a protective substance that lines the esophagus and stomach, reducing the risk of acid reflux and protecting against damage caused by stomach acid.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of comfrey contain a high concentration of allantoin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach and esophagus.
Rhyzomes: Comfrey's rhizomes have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues such as GERD due to their ability to stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh symphytum officinale leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 70°f for 24 hours.
Grind 30 grams of dried symphytum officinale leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Fill 100 gelatin capsules with 200 milligrams each of the powdered herb and seal tightly.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry capsules helps with GERD because they contain antioxidants and anthocyanins that help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach. remedy_properties: Anthocyanins: These powerful antioxidants help reduce inflammation and protect the esophageal lining from damage caused by acid reflux, which can alleviate GERD symptoms.
Fiber: The soluble fiber in blueberries helps absorb excess stomach acid and speed up digestion, reducing the likelihood of acid reflux and GERD episodes.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has anti-inflammatory properties that help relax the lower esophageal sphincter, reducing pressure and preventing acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the blueberry plant contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of GERD.
Barks: The barks of the blueberry plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and improve digestive health, making them effective for treating GERD.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality vaccinium myrtillus berries from a trusted source ensuring optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested berries in a warm oven at 150°f for 24 hours.
Grind 250 grams of dried vaccinium myrtillus into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Mix 1250 milligrams of the powdered vaccinium myrtillus with 500 milligrams of silica gel and 1000 milligrams of calcium carbonate in a bowl.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 500-milligram dosages of the herbal blend prepared in step 4 and store them in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato capsules helps with GERD because they contain a unique compound called Withanolides, which has been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the esophageal lining, alleviating heartburn, nausea, and chest pain.

Antioxidant properties: Herbal Wild Potato Capsules are rich in antioxidants that protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, further reducing inflammation and oxidative stress associated with GERD.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in reducing inflammation and soothing digestive issues associated with GERD.
Rhyzomes: Contain alkaloids that help to reduce stomach acid production, alleviating symptoms of GERD such as heartburn and regurgitation.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 200 grams of dried taraxacum kok saghyz herb into a clean glass bowl.
Combine the herb with 100 grams of dried gentian root and 50 grams of dried lemon balm leaves in the bowl.
Mix well to blend the ingredients for at least 5 minutes until they are thoroughly combined.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal mixture, using approximately 500 milligrams of the blend per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container and refrigerate them for up to 2 years or freeze for up to 5 years.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for GERD are natural supplements containing a blend of herbs that help alleviate symptoms of gastroesophageal reflux disease. These capsules work by reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and enhancing the natural barrier between the stomach and esophagus. The best herbal capsules for GERD include Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Foeniculum vulgare, Zanthoxylum bungeanum, Alpinia galanga, Aloe ferox, Cassia auriculata, Cinnamomum verum, Aloe barbadensis, and Mentha x piperita. If left untreated, GERD can lead to discomfort, pain, and difficulty swallowing, negatively impacting daily life, sleep, and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to GERD that are treated with herbal capsules are heartburn, acid reflux, and digestive disorders. Herbal remedies such as licorice root, marshmallow root, and slippery elm help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort. Other herbs like aloe vera and chamomile may also be used to calm digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with GERD is a blend of Licorice root, Aloe vera, Slippery elm, and Ginger. Licorice root soothes the stomach lining, while Aloe vera reduces inflammation and promotes healing. Slippery elm forms a protective barrier to prevent acid reflux, and Ginger helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing symptoms of GERD. This combination promotes a healthy digestive system, reducing acid production and alleviating discomfort.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Complementary therapies in clinical practice, a randomized double-blind controlled trial was conducted on 70 subjects with gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD) symptoms. The study found that consumption of rose oil soft capsule alleviated cardinal GERD symptoms similar to omeprazole, a proton-pump inhibitor commonly used to treat GERD. This suggests that herbal capsules, specifically rose oil, can be a good alternative for treating GERD symptoms, providing relief without the side effects associated with long-term use of PPIs.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with GERD because they contain licorice root extract, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the stomach lining and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. This helps to alleviate symptoms of acid reflux, such as heartburn and chest pain. Additionally, licorice root has a natural anti-spasmodic effect, which helps to relax the muscles in the esophagus and prevent food from flowing back up into the throat, providing relief from GERD symptoms. It also helps in reducing acid production in the stomach.

Zingiber officinale capsules helps with GERD because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate GERD symptoms, providing relief without the need for medication or surgery.

Regular use of Foeniculum vulgare capsules can provide long-term relief from GERD symptoms.
